metadata.title: Healthier Food Options Across Cuisines in Singapore
metadata.description: A curated guide to healthier dining choices across various cuisines available in Singapore

healthierOptions.dish: Sliced Fish Soup (Hor Fun / Bee Hoon)
healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Low fat, high protein, light broth-based dish with fresh vegetables
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for bee hoon instead of hor fun to reduce calories. Skip the fried fish option.

whereToFind.name: Beach Road Fish Head Bee Hoon

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Customizable - load up on vegetables and tofu. Clear soup base is low in fat.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose clear soup over curry or laksa broth. Pick tofu, leafy greens, mushrooms. Avoid fried items.

whereToFind.name: Hakka Fun HamCha & Yong Tou Fu

healthierOptions.dish: Teochew Porridge with Steamed Fish & Vegetables
healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Light, easy to digest, can pair with steamed dishes and greens
healthierOptions.tips: Choose steamed fish, braised tofu, and leafy vegetables as sides. Avoid fried and preserved items.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Packed with vegetables, tofu, and green tea soup. High fibre, plant-based.
healthierOptions.tips: A naturally healthy Hakka dish. Opt for brown rice if available.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Choose steamed over roasted. Remove skin to cut fat significantly.
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for less rice, extra cucumber. Choose breast meat. Request chilli on the side.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fresh (not fried), filled with vegetables like turnip, beansprouts, and lettuce.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose fresh popiah over fried spring rolls. Ask for less sweet sauce.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Nourishing soups with goji berries, red dates, and herbs. Low fat and hydrating.
healthierOptions.tips: Watercress soup or lotus root soup are great options. Don't finish all the broth (sodium).

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Light turmeric-based broth with noodles, chicken, and beansprouts. Lower fat than most Malay dishes.
healthierOptions.tips: Skip the fried shallots and limit the sambal. Choose yellow noodles over fried noodles.

whereToFind.name: Malay food stalls
whereToFind.location: Geylang Serai Market, Adam Road Food Centre

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Spiced chicken broth with vegetables and rice cakes. Light and nourishing.
healthierOptions.tips: Limit fried toppings. Choose vermicelli over lontong for fewer carbs.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Grilled lean protein. Healthier than fried options when portion-controlled.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose chicken over mutton. Limit peanut sauce. Skip the ketupat (rice cake) or have just one.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Indonesian/Malay salad with blanched vegetables, tofu, and peanut dressing.
healthierOptions.tips: Request less peanut sauce to reduce calories. Rich in fibre and plant protein.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Choose grilled fish, sayur lodeh (vegetable curry), and limit coconut-rich dishes.
healthierOptions.tips: Pick grilled ayam panggang instead of rendang. Choose one vegetable dish, one lean protein. Avoid deep-fried items.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fermented crepe made from rice and lentil batter. Dhal is high protein, low fat.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose plain thosai over masala or paper thosai. Pair with dhal or fish curry (without coconut milk).

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Grilled in clay oven without oil. Yogurt-marinated lean protein.
healthierOptions.tips: Remove the skin. Pair with raita (yogurt) instead of butter-based curries.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Spiced rice with vegetables. Lower in fat than meat biryani with rich gravies.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose vegetable biryani over mutton. Ask for less oil. Pair with raita.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Steamed rice cakes - no oil used in cooking. Sambar is a nutritious lentil-based soup.
healthierOptions.tips: A naturally healthy breakfast option. Light, easy to digest, and filling.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Whole wheat flatbread with less oil than prata. Vegetable curry adds fibre.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose chapati over prata (much less oil). Pair with dhal or dry vegetable curry.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Raw fish is high in omega-3, protein, with zero added fat from cooking.
healthierOptions.tips: Limit soy sauce (sodium). Choose salmon, tuna, or yellowtail for best omega-3 content.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Assorted fresh fish over rice. High protein, rich in omega-3 fatty acids.
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for less rice or brown rice if available. Great protein-to-calorie ratio.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fermented soy base with probiotics. Low calorie, warming, and satisfying.
healthierOptions.tips: Great as a starter to reduce overall meal intake. Contains beneficial probiotics.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Grilled fish with rice, miso soup, and pickles. Balanced macronutrients.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose mackerel (saba) or salmon for omega-3. A well-balanced Japanese set meal.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Buckwheat noodles with more fibre and protein than regular wheat noodles.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose zaru soba (cold) for fewer calories. Tempura on the side adds significant calories.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Whole chicken stuffed with rice, ginseng, and jujubes in clear broth. Nourishing and low fat.
healthierOptions.tips: A traditional Korean health food. Light broth without heavy oils or spice.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Mixed rice bowl with various vegetables, egg, and lean protein. Well-balanced.
healthierOptions.tips: Go easy on the gochujang (chilli paste) and sesame oil. Load up on vegetables.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fermented kimchi provides probiotics. Tofu version is lower in fat.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose tofu-based over pork belly version. Kimchi offers probiotics for gut health.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Sweet potato noodles stir-fried with vegetables and lean meat. Gluten-free.
healthierOptions.tips: Rich in vegetables. Sweet potato noodles are lighter than wheat noodles.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: When shared, provides seafood protein with spring onions. Better than fried chicken appetizers.
healthierOptions.tips: Share as a side dish rather than eating a whole one. Good source of seafood protein.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Spicy and sour clear broth with shrimp, mushrooms, and lemongrass. Low fat.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose Tom Yum Nam Sai (clear) over Nam Khon (creamy). Loaded with herbs and spices.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fresh, raw vegetables with a tangy dressing. High fibre, low calorie.
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for less sugar in the dressing. A refreshing and light starter.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Minced chicken or pork with fresh herbs, lime, and toasted rice. Light and protein-rich.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose chicken larb over pork for less fat. Eat with fresh vegetables instead of sticky rice.

healthierOptions.dish: Steamed Fish with Lime and Chilli
healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Steamed preparation preserves nutrients without added oil. Fresh citrus flavours.
healthierOptions.tips: A much healthier option than deep-fried fish dishes. Rich in protein and omega-3.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Rice noodles with shrimp, tofu, and beansprouts. Moderate when portion-controlled.
healthierOptions.tips: Share a portion. Ask for less oil and sugar. Add extra beansprouts and lime.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Light aromatic broth with rice noodles, lean meat, and fresh herbs. Naturally nutritious.
healthierOptions.tips: Load up on fresh herbs (basil, mint, coriander). Choose lean cuts. Limit hoisin/sriracha.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fresh rice paper rolls with shrimp, vermicelli, and herbs. Not fried.
healthierOptions.tips: Much healthier than fried spring rolls. Dip sparingly in sauce.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Grilled lean pork with fresh herbs, pickled vegetables, and rice vermicelli in light broth.
healthierOptions.tips: Eat with plenty of fresh lettuce and herbs. The light dipping broth is lower in fat than curries.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Grilled meat with pickled vegetables and fresh herbs in a light baguette.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose grilled chicken over pate-heavy versions. Fresh vegetables add fibre and crunch.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Chickpea-based dip rich in protein, fibre, and healthy fats from tahini and olive oil.
healthierOptions.tips: A nutrient-dense option. Chickpeas provide plant protein and fibre.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fresh tomatoes, cucumbers, olives, and feta. Rich in antioxidants and healthy fats.
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for dressing on the side. Olives and olive oil provide heart-healthy monounsaturated fats.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Lean grilled meat with salad and grains. High protein, moderate carbs.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose chicken or lamb shish over doner. Pair with tabbouleh or fattoush for extra vegetables.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Chickpea-based protein, baked or lightly fried. Good plant-based protein source.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose baked falafel if available. Load with salad and tahini instead of mayo.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Fresh fish grilled with olive oil, lemon, and herbs. Rich in omega-3.
healthierOptions.tips: Mediterranean diet staple. Pair with roasted vegetables for a complete meal.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Customizable bowls with lean protein, grains, and vegetables. Balanced macros.
healthierOptions.tips: Choose brown rice or quinoa base. Load up on greens. Go easy on creamy dressings.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Rich in antioxidants, fibre, and healthy fats from nuts and seeds.
healthierOptions.tips: Watch portion size and added granola/honey. Choose fresh fruit toppings.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Lean protein with fresh vegetables. High fibre, low in processed carbs.
healthierOptions.tips: Ask for dressing on the side. Choose vinaigrette over creamy dressings.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Whole grain oats with fruits, nuts, and seeds. Sustained energy release.
healthierOptions.tips: Good breakfast option. Check sugar content in cafe versions.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Pre-portioned balanced meals with lean protein, complex carbs, and vegetables.
healthierOptions.tips: Great for busy professionals. Consistent nutrition with eco-friendly packaging.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Braised mixed vegetables with mushrooms and glass noodles. High fibre, plant-based.
healthierOptions.tips: A naturally vegetable-heavy dish. Good source of dietary fibre.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Chicken braised with buah keluak nuts - rich in minerals. Moderate portions recommended.
healthierOptions.tips: Unique Peranakan dish. Buah keluak nuts are rich in minerals. Share the dish.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Small crispy shells filled with shredded turnip and shrimp. Small portions, balanced flavours.
healthierOptions.tips: Good appetizer option. Small portions help with calorie control.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Creative plant-based cuisine using seasonal vegetables. Low in saturated fat.
healthierOptions.tips: A fine-dining healthy experience. All plant-based ingredients prepared with minimal processing.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Whole grains, legumes, roasted vegetables, and plant protein. Complete nutrition.
healthierOptions.tips: Look for bowls with diverse colours for maximum nutrient variety.

healthierOptions.whyHealthy: Mushrooms are low calorie, high in B vitamins, and provide umami without added fat.
healthierOptions.tips: Great meat substitute. Look for lion's mane, shiitake, or king oyster mushroom dishes.

hpbGuidelines: Look for the Healthier Choice Symbol (HCS) logo at hawker stalls and restaurants
The Healthier Dining Programme (HDP) covers 300+ brands and 2,600+ touchpoints in Singapore
Choose steamed, grilled, or soupy dishes instead of fried
Pick brown rice or wholegrains when available
Watch out for hidden sugars in drinks and sauces
Balance portion sizes - hawker plates are often carb-heavy
Ask for less oil, salt, and sugar when ordering
Choose water, unsweetened tea, or Nutri-Grade A/B drinks

cookingMethodRanking.description: Some oil used but quick cooking preserves nutrients
Can be low fat depending on sauce
High fat, high calories - avoid when possible
